catalysts_full.content:
## TL;DR

Q1 FY2027 earnings prints May 14, 2026 (before market open) — the dominant near-term binary. EPS consensus $0.66. The bull narrative is intact: FY2026 revenue $713.16B (+4.73%) and earnings $21.89B (+12.64%); Q4 beat ($0.74 actual vs. $0.73 estimate). Primary risks are Walton family insider selling ($4.6B) and tariff pass-through from US-China 145% tariffs on Walmart's import-heavy supply chain. Source: run `20260430_212526`.

---

## Upcoming Events

| Event | Date | Direction | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 FY2027 Earnings | May 14, 2026 (BMO) | Binary — bullish setup if beats | EPS consensus $0.66; prior quarter beat $0.74 vs $0.73 |
| Annual Shareholders' Meeting | June 4, 2026 | Neutral | FY2026 annual report released April 30 |
| Quarterly dividend ex-date | May 8, 2026 | Mildly positive | $0.248/share declared |
| FOMC next meeting | June 2026 | Moderate — no cuts priced | Rate-flat environment pressures consumer discretionary; WMT defensive benefits |
| US-China tariff resolution | Ongoing | High impact | 80% of suppliers in China; 145% tariff rate is structural headwind |

---

## Recent News Synthesis (as of April 30)

**Bullish:**
- **Morgan Stanley raised PT to $140** (from $135), Overweight maintained (April 22, 2026). Consensus: 28 Buy, 2 Hold, 0 Sell out of 27-48 analysts; avg target $137.60. (Source: `20260430_212526/web_research/WMT.json`)
- **FY2026 results strong:** Revenue $713.16B (+4.73% YoY), earnings $21.89B (+12.64% YoY). Q4 FY2026 EPS $0.74 beat estimate $0.73. E-commerce US comps +27% YoY; first-ever profitable e-commerce quarter globally. (Source: `20260430_212526/web_research/WMT.json`)
- **Quarterly dividend declared:** $0.248/share, ex-date May 8, 2026. Consistent capital return. (Source: `20260430_212526/web_research/WMT.json`)
- **Great Value private brand redesign:** 650+ store remodels, ~20 new locations, ~10,000 private-label items relaunched — supports gross margin improvement. (Source: `20260430_212526/web_research/WMT.json`)
- **Leadership pipeline investment:** Store manager compensation expanded to $119K-$276K including bonuses — retention and talent quality signal. (Source: `20260430_212526/web_research/WMT.json`)

**Bearish / Neutral:**
- **$4.23B debt offering** (five-tranche senior unsecured notes, maturities 2029-2036, announced April 27, 2026). Increases leverage modestly; rated investment-grade but signals balance sheet deployment. (Source: `20260430_212526/web_research/WMT.json`)
- **Walton family insider selling: $4.6B** (cumulative figure cited across multiple runs). Founding family treating current price as a distribution opportunity. (Source: `20260430_212526/signals_combined.json`, swing_catalyst_news)
- **HSBC downgraded** Feb 20, 2026 — sole major bank bear move; sole Hold in the consensus. (Source: `20260430_212526/web_research/WMT.json`)
- **Tariff headwind structural:** US-China 145% tariff rate affects ~80% of suppliers. Walmart pressuring suppliers to absorb costs — adversarial dynamic. India/Vietnam sourcing shift is a multi-year project. (Source: `20260430_212526/web_research/WMT.json`)
- **Costco grocery share risk:** Costco prices 21.4% below Walmart in grocery, gaining market share faster than any major rival. (Source: `20260430_212526/web_research/WMT.json`)

---

## Analyst Consensus

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Rating | Buy consensus |
| Buy / Hold / Sell | 28 / 2 / 0 |
| Avg price target | $137.60 |
| Target range | $112 – $150 |
| Most recent change | Morgan Stanley raised to $140 (Apr 22); HSBC downgraded to Hold (Feb 20) |

lessons_full.content:
## Format

Each lesson is one dated bullet:

`[DATE] | [TICKER] | [SETUP TYPE] | [OUTCOME: +$X / -$X] | [WHY it worked or failed in 1 sentence]`

The "why" should reference: the thesis at the time, the macro regime, which swing strategy recommended it.

---

## Patterns (auto-generated by Sunday compactor, last 30 days)

| Setup Type | Trades | Wins | Win Rate | Avg P&L | Total P&L |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| EMA pullback Fib dip-buy | 1 | 0 | 0% | -$63.20 | -$63.20 |

## Lessons

2026-04-30 | NVDA | EMA pullback Fib dip-buy | -$63.20 (corrected from -$264.65) | Stop triggered at $205.30 (hourly Fib 61.8%); swing_trend_momentum and swing_breakout were bullish on AI capex thesis, but price breached the stop zone intraday amid stretched macro (RSI 81–87) with no bullish confirmation candle before exit. **Note:** trade was originally sized 67 shares ($14,020) due to a phantom $100K budget bug (fixed commit b2b472d). With the correct $25K budget at 14.2% vol-adj limit, position would have been 16 shares ($3,348). Corrected P&L reflects the strategy's actual signal quality, not the sizing error.
